message.text: Inline policy inline_user_policy is attached directly to an IAM user. Policies should be attached to groups; direct user attachment is hard to audit.
IAM policy policy grants full administrative privileges (Action "*" on Resource "*"). Any principal assuming this policy has full account control.
IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy grants full administrative privileges (Action "*" on Resource "*"). Any principal assuming this policy has full account control.
S3 bucket policy on CloudTrailS3Bucket-Policy does not reference aws:SecureTransport — traffic can potentially be accepted over plain HTTP.
S3 bucket logging: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket access_logging: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket main: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket logging: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket getonly: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.
S3 bucket public: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.

RDS instance main: deletion_protection is disabled. An accidental terraform destroy will permanently delete the database.
RDS Performance Insights uses AWS-managed key (not CMK): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier
RDS instance has Enhanced Monitoring disabled (monitoring_interval = 0): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier
EC2 instance should have EBS root volume encryption enabled: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: root_block_device.encrypted
EC2 instance main: no metadata_options block declared. AWS defaults to http_tokens = "optional" (IMDSv1 enabled). SSRF-based credential theft via the instance metadata service remains possible.
KMS key deletion window should be the maximum (30 days): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: deletion_window_in_days
KMS key deletion window should be the maximum (30 days): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: deletion_window_in_days
ACM certificate should have key_algorithm specified (not default): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: key_algorithm
CloudTrail main: is_multi_region_trail is false. API activity in other regions is unlogged — any attacker who pivots regions becomes invisible.
CloudTrail duplicate: is_multi_region_trail is false. API activity in other regions is unlogged — any attacker who pivots regions becomes invisible.
CloudTrail duplicate: log file integrity validation is disabled. Tampered log files cannot be detected.
CloudTrail main: no CloudWatch Logs group is attached. Near-real-time alerting on suspicious API activity is not possible.
CloudTrail duplicate: no CloudWatch Logs group is attached. Near-real-time alerting on suspicious API activity is not possible.
ECR repository main: scan_on_push is not enabled. Pushed images are not automatically scanned for OS-package CVEs.
ECR repository main: image tags are mutable. The same tag (e.g., `latest`, `v1.2.3`) can point at different image SHAs over time — supply-chain attacks become invisible.
ECR repository main: no aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy references this repository. Old image versions accumulate forever — at $0.10/GB/month, hot CI repos can grow to $200-1000/month before anyone notices.
EKS cluster example: no encryption_config block. Kubernetes Secrets are stored unencrypted in etcd — anyone with etcd-level access (or backup access) reads them in plaintext.
VPC main: no aws_flow_log resource references this VPC. Network-level forensics (lateral movement, exfiltration) are unreliable post-incident.

Subnet main: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

Security group all_ports_to_all: ingress rule opens all ports (from_port = 0) to the internet (0.0.0.0/0). This exposes every listening service on attached resources — SSH, database ports, admin UIs, debug endpoints — to the public internet.

Security group all_ports_to_self: ingress rule opens all ports (from_port = 0) to the internet (0.0.0.0/0). This exposes every listening service on attached resources — SSH, database ports, admin UIs, debug endpoints — to the public internet.

Security group icmp_to_all: ingress rule opens all ports (from_port = 0) to the internet (0.0.0.0/0). This exposes every listening service on attached resources — SSH, database ports, admin UIs, debug endpoints — to the public internet.
Redshift parameter group main: no require_ssl=1 parameter declared. The cluster accepts plaintext psql connections — query data and credentials cross the wire unencrypted.
Redshift cluster main: audit logging disabled. Forensic investigation of who-ran-what, including credential-misuse cases, is impossible after the fact.
SNS topic main: no kms_master_key_id. Messages at rest in the SNS persistence layer rely on the AWS-managed key — no CMK rotation, no audit boundary.
SQS queue main: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
RDS 'throwaway-prod' configuration smell: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier
ALB target group deregistration delay exceeds connection draining budget: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
RDS apply_immediately = true (destructive changes outside maintenance window): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier
ALB has no CloudWatch alarm on UnHealthyHostCount: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
RDS instance has no CloudWatch alarm on CPU or DatabaseConnections: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

SQS queue main: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.
Log group main: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.
RDS engine_version pinned to major version only: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: engine_version
Public ALB has no Route53 health check on its alias: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
Load balancer has deletion_protection disabled: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
EKS cluster example: <= 1 aws_eks_node_group references this cluster. Single-pool clusters can't drain workloads across pools during a kernel upgrade or instance-family migration.
RDS main: max_allocated_storage unset. The instance can't grow beyond its allocated_storage value. A surprise write spike → ENOSPC → forced downtime to bump storage manually.
IAM role has no permissions_boundary: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
IAM policy inline_user_policy: uses NotAction. New AWS services launched after this policy is written are implicitly granted to whatever principal uses it.
IAM policy inline_role_policy: uses NotAction. New AWS services launched after this policy is written are implicitly granted to whatever principal uses it.
IAM role allow_all: assume_role_policy allows Principal: AWS: "*". Any AWS account can call sts:AssumeRole into your account, get whatever permissions this role has.
IAM role allow_all_and_no_mfa: assume_role_policy allows Principal: AWS: "*". Any AWS account can call sts:AssumeRole into your account, get whatever permissions this role has.

CloudWatch Log Group main: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.
logging: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.
access_logging: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.
main: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.

main: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.

VPC main: no aws_vpc_endpoint declared. Secrets Manager calls from in-VPC workloads traverse NAT. At ~$0.045/GB through NAT, even modest secret-fetching patterns pay back the endpoint cost in days.
VPC main: no aws_vpc_endpoint for KMS. Decrypt calls leave the VPC via NAT — for high-volume workloads this adds up faster than the endpoint cost.
Cluster example: no aws_vpc_endpoint declared for ECR or S3. Container image pulls + Docker layer downloads flow through NAT — common single-biggest contributor to NAT data-processing charges.
main: no aws_inspector2_enabler in the plan. Container / runtime / EC2 CVEs aren't surfaced — the team is doing vulnerability management manually or not at all.
example: no aws_inspector2_enabler in the plan. Container / runtime / EC2 CVEs aren't surfaced — the team is doing vulnerability management manually or not at all.
Load balancer main: access logging is not enabled. Request-level forensics (source IP, path, status, latency, backend) are unavailable after an incident — you cannot reconstruct who hit what.
Load balancer has no access logging: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
Classic ELB main: no HTTPS/SSL listener — every listener is plaintext HTTP/TCP. Traffic between clients and the load balancer, including session tokens, crosses the wire unencrypted.
ALB main: drop_invalid_header_fields is false. Malformed HTTP headers (e.g. header injection, request smuggling via ambiguous Content-Length) are forwarded to backend targets unchanged.
OpenSearch domain main: at-rest encryption is disabled. Index data, snapshots, and logs are stored in cleartext on the underlying EBS volumes.
OpenSearch domain main: HTTPS is not enforced. Queries and documents are transmitted in cleartext — credentials and data are interceptable.
OpenSearch domain main: node-to-node encryption is disabled. Intra-cluster shard replication traffic is unencrypted — interceptable within the VPC.
OpenSearch domain main: fine-grained access control (FGAC) is disabled. All authenticated requests have full read/write access to every index — no per-index or per-document authorization.
OpenSearch domain main: audit logging is not enabled. Authentication events, index access, and privileged operations are not recorded.

CloudWatch log group main: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.

IAM policy inline_group_policy: enables privilege escalation — broad NotAction grant (allows all actions but a few) — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

IAM policy inline_user_policy: enables privilege escalation — broad NotAction grant (allows all actions but a few) — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

IAM policy inline_role_policy: enables privilege escalation — broad NotAction grant (allows all actions but a few) — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

IAM policy policy: enables privilege escalation — full administrator wildcard (Action: "*") — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy: enables privilege escalation — full administrator wildcard (Action: "*") — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

IAM policy main: grants data exfiltration actions (s3:GetObject) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can bulk-read or export data across all accessible resources of that service — without needing to escalate privileges first.

IAM policy policy: grants data exfiltration actions (s3:GetObject, dynamodb:Scan, dynamodb:GetItem, dynamodb:BatchGetItem, dynamodb:Query, kinesis:GetRecords, sqs:ReceiveMessage, rds:DownloadDBLogFilePortion, athena:GetQueryResults, glue:GetSecurityConfiguration) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can bulk-read or export data across all accessible resources of that service — without needing to escalate privileges first.

IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy: grants data exfiltration actions (s3:GetObject, dynamodb:Scan, dynamodb:GetItem, dynamodb:BatchGetItem, dynamodb:Query, kinesis:GetRecords, sqs:ReceiveMessage, rds:DownloadDBLogFilePortion, athena:GetQueryResults, glue:GetSecurityConfiguration) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can bulk-read or export data across all accessible resources of that service — without needing to escalate privileges first.

IAM policy policy: grants credential extraction actions (secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:BatchGetSecretValue, ssm:GetParameter, ssm:GetParameters, ssm:GetParametersByPath, kms:Decrypt, kms:GenerateDataKey, sts:GetSessionToken, sts:GetFederationToken) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can read secrets, SSM parameters, or decrypt KMS-protected data across all resources in the account.

IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy: grants credential extraction actions (secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:BatchGetSecretValue, ssm:GetParameter, ssm:GetParameters, ssm:GetParametersByPath, kms:Decrypt, kms:GenerateDataKey, sts:GetSessionToken, sts:GetFederationToken) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can read secrets, SSM parameters, or decrypt KMS-protected data across all resources in the account.

IAM policy main: grants resource-exposure actions (s3:PutBucketPolicy, s3:PutBucketAcl, s3:PutObjectAcl) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can modify the access policy of S3 buckets, Lambda functions, SQS queues, KMS keys, or other resources — potentially making them publicly accessible.

IAM policy policy: grants resource-exposure actions (s3:PutBucketPolicy, s3:PutBucketAcl, s3:PutObjectAcl, lambda:AddPermission, lambda:AddLayerVersionPermission, sqs:AddPermission, sns:AddPermission, secretsmanager:PutResourcePolicy, kms:PutKeyPolicy, ecr:SetRepositoryPolicy) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can modify the access policy of S3 buckets, Lambda functions, SQS queues, KMS keys, or other resources — potentially making them publicly accessible.

IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy: grants resource-exposure actions (s3:PutBucketPolicy, s3:PutBucketAcl, s3:PutObjectAcl, lambda:AddPermission, lambda:AddLayerVersionPermission, sqs:AddPermission, sns:AddPermission, secretsmanager:PutResourcePolicy, kms:PutKeyPolicy, ecr:SetRepositoryPolicy) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can modify the access policy of S3 buckets, Lambda functions, SQS queues, KMS keys, or other resources — potentially making them publicly accessible.

IAM policy policy: grants infrastructure-modification actions (ec2:RunInstances, ec2:RequestSpotInstances, ecs:RunTask, eks:CreateCluster, rds:CreateDBInstance, rds:RestoreDBInstanceFromDBSnapshot, ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress, ec2:CreateVpc, ec2:CreateKeyPair) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can launch EC2 instances, run ECS tasks, or modify network rules without restriction — a common cryptomining and lateral-movement vector.

IAM policy admin_not_indicated_policy: grants infrastructure-modification actions (ec2:RunInstances, ec2:RequestSpotInstances, ecs:RunTask, eks:CreateCluster, rds:CreateDBInstance, rds:RestoreDBInstanceFromDBSnapshot, ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress, ec2:CreateVpc, ec2:CreateKeyPair) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can launch EC2 instances, run ECS tasks, or modify network rules without restriction — a common cryptomining and lateral-movement vector.

KMS key exposed: key policy has Principal = "*" with Allow for [kms:*] and no Condition. Any AWS identity (including principals in other accounts) can use this key to decrypt data, generate data keys, or create grants — effectively making encryption with this key meaningless.

Elasticsearch domain `main` has no vpc_options (public endpoint) and advanced_security_options.enabled is not true. Without fine-grained access control, anonymous HTTP requests can reach the cluster's REST API — enabling full index read/write, cluster configuration, and snapshot exfiltration from any internet host.

`aws_instance.main` attribute `user_data` contains what looks like a hardcoded AWS access key (pattern: var.…). Access key IDs embedded in Terraform plans appear in state files, CI logs, and any system that runs `terraform show`.

`aws_db_instance.main` sets `password` to a literal string value. The database master password will appear in Terraform state (possibly in S3) and in CI/CD logs for the duration of the resource's life. Use `random_password` (Terraform-generated, stored in state) or a Secrets Manager reference instead.

`aws_redshift_cluster.main` sets `master_password` to a literal string value. The database master password will appear in Terraform state (possibly in S3) and in CI/CD logs for the duration of the resource's life. Use `random_password` (Terraform-generated, stored in state) or a Secrets Manager reference instead.

Plan includes hourly-billed resources (1 aws_db_instance, 1 aws_eks_cluster, 1 aws_instance, 1 aws_redshift_cluster) but no aws_budgets_budget. Without a cost alarm, a forgotten instance, misconfigured autoscaling, or credential compromise can run up an unbounded bill before the team notices.

Log group (main) has no retention_in_days set. CloudWatch keeps logs forever by default at $0.03/GB-month. A high-traffic service generating 10 GB/day accumulates ~$110/month in storage after one year with no bound in sight.

S3 buckets (logging, access_logging, main, logging, getonly, public) have no aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration. Without lifecycle rules, objects accumulate forever: old versions from versioning, abandoned multipart uploads, and stale data all bill at $0.023/GB-month with no bound.
Role is declared but no other resource references it. Either delete it or document why it's kept (e.g., AssumeRole from another account).

IAM role main: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.

aws_iam_user inline_user declared. Each IAM user is a long-lived credential surface. Legitimate cases: external system integrations that don't support IRSA / OIDC federation. Humans should go through Identity Center; pods should use IRSA; CI/CD should use OIDC.

fullDescription.text: ALB main: drop_invalid_header_fields is false. Malformed HTTP headers (e.g. header injection, request smuggling via ambiguous Content-Length) are forwarded to backend targets unchanged.

shortDescription.text: ALB does not drop invalid HTTP header fields

fullDescription.text: Inline policy inline_user_policy is attached directly to an IAM user. Policies should be attached to groups; direct user attachment is hard to audit.

shortDescription.text: IAM users should not have inline policies attached

fullDescription.text: IAM policy policy grants full administrative privileges (Action "*" on Resource "*"). Any principal assuming this policy has full account control.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy should not grant full administrative privileges (Action * Resource *)

fullDescription.text: IAM policy inline_group_policy: enables privilege escalation — broad NotAction grant (allows all actions but a few) — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy enables privilege escalation

fullDescription.text: IAM policy main: grants data exfiltration actions (s3:GetObject) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can bulk-read or export data across all accessible resources of that service — without needing to escalate privileges first.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy grants bulk data exfiltration capability

fullDescription.text: IAM policy policy: grants credential extraction actions (secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:BatchGetSecretValue, ssm:GetParameter, ssm:GetParameters, ssm:GetParametersByPath, kms:Decrypt, kms:GenerateDataKey, sts:GetSessionToken, sts:GetFederationToken) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can read secrets, SSM parameters, or decrypt KMS-protected data across all resources in the account.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy grants credential/secret extraction capability

fullDescription.text: IAM policy main: grants resource-exposure actions (s3:PutBucketPolicy, s3:PutBucketAcl, s3:PutObjectAcl) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can modify the access policy of S3 buckets, Lambda functions, SQS queues, KMS keys, or other resources — potentially making them publicly accessible.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy grants resource permission modification capability

fullDescription.text: IAM policy policy: grants infrastructure-modification actions (ec2:RunInstances, ec2:RequestSpotInstances, ecs:RunTask, eks:CreateCluster, rds:CreateDBInstance, rds:RestoreDBInstanceFromDBSnapshot, ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress, ec2:CreateVpc, ec2:CreateKeyPair) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can launch EC2 instances, run ECS tasks, or modify network rules without restriction — a common cryptomining and lateral-movement vector.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy grants unscoped infrastructure launch/modification capability

fullDescription.text: KMS key deletion window should be the maximum (30 days): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: deletion_window_in_days

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] KMS key deletion window should be the maximum (30 days)

fullDescription.text: KMS key exposed: key policy has Principal = "*" with Allow for [kms:*] and no Condition. Any AWS identity (including principals in other accounts) can use this key to decrypt data, generate data keys, or create grants — effectively making encryption with this key meaningless.

shortDescription.text: KMS key policy grants cryptographic access to any AWS principal

fullDescription.text: CloudWatch log group main: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.

shortDescription.text: CloudWatch log group has no retention policy — logs accumulate indefinitely

fullDescription.text: OpenSearch domain main: at-rest encryption is disabled. Index data, snapshots, and logs are stored in cleartext on the underlying EBS volumes.

shortDescription.text: OpenSearch domain does not encrypt data at rest

fullDescription.text: OpenSearch domain main: HTTPS is not enforced. Queries and documents are transmitted in cleartext — credentials and data are interceptable.

shortDescription.text: OpenSearch domain does not enforce HTTPS

fullDescription.text: OpenSearch domain main: node-to-node encryption is disabled. Intra-cluster shard replication traffic is unencrypted — interceptable within the VPC.

shortDescription.text: OpenSearch domain does not encrypt node-to-node traffic

fullDescription.text: OpenSearch domain main: fine-grained access control (FGAC) is disabled. All authenticated requests have full read/write access to every index — no per-index or per-document authorization.

shortDescription.text: OpenSearch domain has fine-grained access control disabled

fullDescription.text: OpenSearch domain main: audit logging is not enabled. Authentication events, index access, and privileged operations are not recorded.

shortDescription.text: OpenSearch domain audit logging is not enabled

fullDescription.text: RDS 'throwaway-prod' configuration smell: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS 'throwaway-prod' configuration smell

fullDescription.text: ALB target group deregistration delay exceeds connection draining budget: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] ALB target group deregistration delay exceeds connection draining budget

fullDescription.text: RDS apply_immediately = true (destructive changes outside maintenance window): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS apply_immediately = true (destructive changes outside maintenance window)

fullDescription.text: ALB has no CloudWatch alarm on UnHealthyHostCount: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] ALB has no CloudWatch alarm on UnHealthyHostCount

fullDescription.text: RDS instance has no CloudWatch alarm on CPU or DatabaseConnections: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS instance has no CloudWatch alarm on CPU or DatabaseConnections

fullDescription.text: SQS queue main: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

shortDescription.text: SQS queue has no alarm on ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage

fullDescription.text: Log group main: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.

shortDescription.text: CloudWatch log group has no retention_in_days set (logs retained forever)

fullDescription.text: RDS engine_version pinned to major version only: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: engine_version

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS engine_version pinned to major version only

fullDescription.text: Public ALB has no Route53 health check on its alias: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] Public ALB has no Route53 health check on its alias

fullDescription.text: Load balancer has deletion_protection disabled: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] Load balancer has deletion_protection disabled

fullDescription.text: EKS cluster example: <= 1 aws_eks_node_group references this cluster. Single-pool clusters can't drain workloads across pools during a kernel upgrade or instance-family migration.

shortDescription.text: EKS cluster declares a single node group

fullDescription.text: RDS main: max_allocated_storage unset. The instance can't grow beyond its allocated_storage value. A surprise write spike → ENOSPC → forced downtime to bump storage manually.

shortDescription.text: RDS instance has no max_allocated_storage (no storage autoscaling)

fullDescription.text: IAM role has no permissions_boundary: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] IAM role has no permissions_boundary

fullDescription.text: aws_iam_user inline_user declared. Each IAM user is a long-lived credential surface. Legitimate cases: external system integrations that don't support IRSA / OIDC federation. Humans should go through Identity Center; pods should use IRSA; CI/CD should use OIDC.

shortDescription.text: IAM user resource declared (use Identity Center / SSO instead)

fullDescription.text: IAM policy inline_user_policy: uses NotAction. New AWS services launched after this policy is written are implicitly granted to whatever principal uses it.

shortDescription.text: IAM policy document uses NotAction (broad permissions)

fullDescription.text: IAM role allow_all: assume_role_policy allows Principal: AWS: "*". Any AWS account can call sts:AssumeRole into your account, get whatever permissions this role has.

shortDescription.text: IAM role trust policy allows Principal: AWS: "*"

fullDescription.text: IAM role with inline policy (should be managed policy): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] IAM role with inline policy (should be managed policy)

fullDescription.text: Load balancer declared without any target groups attached: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] Load balancer declared without any target groups attached

fullDescription.text: CloudWatch Log Group main: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.

shortDescription.text: CloudWatch Log Group has no retention policy — logs accumulate indefinitely

fullDescription.text: logging: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.

shortDescription.text: Stateful resource missing lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }

fullDescription.text: main: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.

shortDescription.text: Resource uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` (should be for_each)

fullDescription.text: Variable "vpc_id": no `type` declaration and no validation block. Mistakes surface as cryptic provider errors instead of clear plan-time messages.

shortDescription.text: Variable declared without type or validation

fullDescription.text: VPC main: no aws_vpc_endpoint declared. Secrets Manager calls from in-VPC workloads traverse NAT. At ~$0.045/GB through NAT, even modest secret-fetching patterns pay back the endpoint cost in days.

shortDescription.text: VPC has no Secrets Manager VPC endpoint (Lambda → Secrets traffic via NAT)

fullDescription.text: VPC main: no aws_vpc_endpoint for KMS. Decrypt calls leave the VPC via NAT — for high-volume workloads this adds up faster than the endpoint cost.

shortDescription.text: VPC has no KMS VPC endpoint

fullDescription.text: Cluster example: no aws_vpc_endpoint declared for ECR or S3. Container image pulls + Docker layer downloads flow through NAT — common single-biggest contributor to NAT data-processing charges.

shortDescription.text: EKS / ECS cluster present but no ECR VPC endpoints

fullDescription.text: main: no aws_inspector2_enabler in the plan. Container / runtime / EC2 CVEs aren't surfaced — the team is doing vulnerability management manually or not at all.

shortDescription.text: No aws_inspector2_enabler declared (no vulnerability scanning)

fullDescription.text: RDS instance main: deletion_protection is disabled. An accidental terraform destroy will permanently delete the database.

shortDescription.text: RDS deletion protection should be enabled

fullDescription.text: RDS Performance Insights uses AWS-managed key (not CMK): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS Performance Insights uses AWS-managed key (not CMK)

fullDescription.text: RDS instance has Enhanced Monitoring disabled (monitoring_interval = 0): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: identifier

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] RDS instance has Enhanced Monitoring disabled (monitoring_interval = 0)

fullDescription.text: Redshift parameter group main: no require_ssl=1 parameter declared. The cluster accepts plaintext psql connections — query data and credentials cross the wire unencrypted.

shortDescription.text: Redshift parameter group does not enforce SSL connections

fullDescription.text: Redshift cluster main: audit logging disabled. Forensic investigation of who-ran-what, including credential-misuse cases, is impossible after the fact.

shortDescription.text: Redshift cluster does not enable user-activity logging

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket policy on CloudTrailS3Bucket-Policy does not reference aws:SecureTransport — traffic can potentially be accepted over plain HTTP.

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket policy should deny non-TLS (HTTP) access

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket uses a legacy public ACL (inline acl attribute): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: bucket

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] S3 bucket uses a legacy public ACL (inline acl attribute)

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket logging: no aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block references this bucket. Public access posture is one mis-applied policy or ACL away.

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket has no Block Public Access companion resource

fullDescription.text: S3 audit/compliance bucket has no object-lock configuration: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: bucket

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] S3 audit/compliance bucket has no object-lock configuration

fullDescription.text: SNS topic main: no kms_master_key_id. Messages at rest in the SNS persistence layer rely on the AWS-managed key — no CMK rotation, no audit boundary.

shortDescription.text: SNS topic uses AWS-managed key (not CMK) for encryption

fullDescription.text: SQS queue main: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.

shortDescription.text: SQS queue has no DLQ — failed messages drop silently

fullDescription.text: VPC main: no aws_flow_log resource references this VPC. Network-level forensics (lateral movement, exfiltration) are unreliable post-incident.

shortDescription.text: VPC has no associated flow logs

fullDescription.text: Subnet main: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

shortDescription.text: VPC subnet automatically assigns public IPs on launch

fullDescription.text: Security group all_ports_to_all: ingress rule opens all ports (from_port = 0) to the internet (0.0.0.0/0). This exposes every listening service on attached resources — SSH, database ports, admin UIs, debug endpoints — to the public internet.

shortDescription.text: Security group allows unrestricted ingress on all ports from the internet

fullDescription.text: Role is declared but no other resource references it. Either delete it or document why it's kept (e.g., AssumeRole from another account).

shortDescription.text: IAM role allow_all has no consumers

fullDescription.text: Security group is declared but no resource attaches it. Likely a leftover from a deleted service.

shortDescription.text: Security group all_ports_to_all has no consumers

fullDescription.text: Messages published to this topic disappear silently — no subscriber receives them.

shortDescription.text: SNS topic main has no subscriptions
